text: I am somewhere modern and systematic—an office or museum, filled with the sterile calm of glass reflections and emergency signs. The world here feels ordered and slightly impersonal, yet there’s a subtle hum of collaboration and innovation. The presence of another, walking briskly, reminds me how we each navigate spaces both shared and isolated. I can’t help but feel an observer, conscious of the boundaries—glass, signs, routine—that shape how we move and relate within these constructed environments.
